notes: Alcohol can be brought into India within duty-free limits although rules can vary by state
Food items can be brought into India but restrictions apply to some agricultural and animal products
Cash can be brought into India, but foreign currency above certain thresholds must be declared.
Duty-free allowances apply in India for alcohol, tobacco, and goods within specific limits.
Personal electronics can be brought into India, but high-value goods may be subject to duty.
Medication can be brought into India for personal use, but controlled drugs require proper documentation.
Tobacco can be brought into India within duty-free limits for personal use.
Weapons are generally prohibited from being brought into India without strict authorization.
Prescription drugs can be carried into India for personal use with appropriate documentation.
You must declare certain goods when entering India, especially high-value items, cash, and restricted goods.
